terms.definition: Assistive device with a trigger-operated grasper used to retrieve items without bending or stretching.
terms.exampleSentence: A long-handled reacher was provided for picking up objects from the floor post hip replacement.
terms.contextNotes: Standard post-hip-replacement kit item.

terms.definition: Plastic or fabric cradle that allows a sock to be put on without bending to the foot.
terms.exampleSentence: She demonstrated independent dressing using a sock aid and long shoehorn.
terms.contextNotes: Common after hip or knee surgery.

terms.definition: Removable seat that increases toilet height to ease sit-to-stand and reduce hip flexion.
terms.exampleSentence: A raised toilet seat with armrests was fitted before discharge.
terms.contextNotes: Specify height (2/4/6 inch) on order.

terms.definition: Stable seat used inside the shower or wet room to allow safe seated washing.
terms.exampleSentence: A shower chair with backrest was provided due to fatigue and poor balance.
terms.contextNotes: Check chair fits cubicle and weight limit.

terms.definition: High stool with sloped seat that supports semi-standing for kitchen or bathroom tasks.
terms.exampleSentence: A perching stool enabled meal preparation while managing breathlessness.
terms.contextNotes: Useful in COPD and cardiac rehab.

terms.definition: Fixed handhold installed on walls to provide support during transfers in bathrooms or stairs.
terms.exampleSentence: Two grab rails were fitted beside the toilet and bath.
terms.contextNotes: Specify length, position and angle on referral.

terms.definition: Pressure-redistributing cushion prescribed to maintain skin integrity and posture in seated users.
terms.exampleSentence: A high-risk wheelchair cushion was issued following sacral redness.
terms.contextNotes: Match cushion grade to pressure-ulcer risk.

terms.definition: Static orthosis worn at rest to position the hand and wrist in a neutral, functional posture.
terms.exampleSentence: A night-time resting splint was fabricated to prevent wrist contracture.
terms.contextNotes: Document wear schedule and skin checks.

terms.definition: Electronic or low-tech tool that supports communication when speech is limited or absent.
terms.exampleSentence: An eye-gaze AAC device was trialled for the patient with locked-in syndrome.
terms.contextNotes: Joint OT/SLT assessment recommended.

terms.definition: Coordinated small-muscle movements of the hand needed for tasks such as writing or fastenings.
terms.exampleSentence: Fine motor skills were targeted with bead threading and pencil control tasks.
terms.contextNotes: Common paediatric referral reason.

terms.definition: Movements involving large muscle groups for posture, locomotion and whole-body coordination.
terms.exampleSentence: Gross motor skills were addressed through obstacle courses and ball games.
terms.contextNotes: Often co-assessed with physiotherapy.

terms.definition: A child's developmental readiness for the demands of formal schooling, including self-care and attention.
terms.exampleSentence: School readiness goals included independent toileting and using cutlery.
terms.contextNotes: Coordinate with early years team.

terms.definition: A satisfying mix of self-care, productivity and leisure occupations across a person's day or week.
terms.exampleSentence: Restoring occupational balance was a key goal in the burnout management plan.
terms.contextNotes: Useful concept in mental health OT.

terms.definition: Coordinated set of community services arranged to meet a person's care needs at home.
terms.exampleSentence: A package of care of four daily visits was arranged on discharge.
terms.contextNotes: Often abbreviated POC in handovers.

terms.definition: Pre- or post-discharge visit to assess the home environment and ensure safe transition.
terms.exampleSentence: A pre-discharge home visit identified the need for a stair rail.
terms.contextNotes: Document layout, hazards and equipment needs.

terms.definition: Multidisciplinary team comprising clinicians from different professions collaborating on patient care.
terms.exampleSentence: The case was discussed at the weekly MDT meeting.
terms.contextNotes: Spell out on first written use.

terms.definition: Scheduled multidisciplinary discussion to coordinate the patient's safe transfer from hospital to community.
terms.exampleSentence: Equipment and care needs were agreed at the discharge planning meeting.
terms.contextNotes: Document outcomes and actions clearly.

terms.definition: Process of assessing for, ordering and supplying assistive equipment to support function at home.
terms.exampleSentence: Equipment provision included a perching stool and bath board.
terms.contextNotes: Routed via community equipment service.

terms.definition: Cushion designed to redistribute pressure and reduce risk of pressure ulcers in seated users.
terms.exampleSentence: A pressure-relieving cushion was issued in line with her Waterlow score.
terms.contextNotes: Match grade to risk and tissue viability advice.

terms.definition: Mechanical device used to lift and transfer a person who cannot weight-bear safely.
terms.exampleSentence: Two-person hoist transfers were required from bed to chair.
terms.contextNotes: Specify sling size and type in care plan.

terms.definition: Low-friction fabric sheet used to reposition or transfer a patient with reduced effort and shear.
terms.exampleSentence: Slide sheets were used for repositioning to protect the sacral pressure area.
terms.contextNotes: Single-patient use to reduce infection risk.

terms.definition: Handrail fitted alongside stairs to support safe ascent and descent.
terms.exampleSentence: A second stair rail was installed to enable independent stair use.
terms.contextNotes: Note stair count and side requested.

terms.definition: Rigid board placed across the bath to provide a seated transfer surface for safer bathing.
terms.exampleSentence: A bath board was provided as a temporary solution pending wet-room conversion.
terms.contextNotes: Confirm bath width before issue.

terms.definition: Short-term, goal-focused service helping people regain skills and confidence after illness or hospital stay.
terms.exampleSentence: She was referred to reablement for six weeks of personal-care support.
terms.contextNotes: Typically time-limited and free at point of use.

terms.definition: Pathway moving medically fit patients home or to a community setting before completing assessments.
terms.exampleSentence: She was placed on the discharge to assess pathway with same-day OT review.
terms.contextNotes: Reduces length of stay; needs robust community follow-up.
